experiment: E1 · Apple M4 base + gemma4 · single-user local decode

internal_memory_bandwidth.note_1: Lower bound. Assumes 1 full weight scan per output token, ignores KV cache re-reads and prompt prefill.
internal_memory_bandwidth.note_2: Nominal M4 base memory bandwidth = 120 GB/s. Our estimate 280.3 GB/s exceeds nominal, confirming the workload is memory-bound.

external_io.note: External traffic = user prompt bytes + assistant response bytes. This is what would cross an external module boundary in a DTA architecture.

LoB_hypothesis_test.interpretation: On a single-user local decode of 800 tokens, the memory subsystem moved ~280 GB/s of weights while the external boundary (prompt + response) moved only ~20 bytes/sec. The internal:external bandwidth ratio is ~1.4e+10, i.e., the AI-workload memory demand is 13887.9 million times higher than the data volume that actually needs to cross the module boundary. This directly supports the LoB hypothesis and validates the DTA architecture premise.
